Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Im planning to structure the analysis by identifying anomalies in the data developing hypotheses about whats causing them and then writing PostgreSQL queries to verify those hypotheses

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: I need to be careful about how the claim_events and adjusters tables relate to each other it looks like the connection might be through the resource field matching adjuster names or possibly through additional_info so Ill note that assumption as I work through this

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: For the verification queries Im building a series of CTEs that will extract the first occurrence of each activity per claim compute transition deltas and z scores against a baseline profile then run checks for structural violations like missing intermediate activities ordering problems duplicate events and timestamp anomalies

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Im also planning to look for batch patterns in notifications and correlate findings by claim type resource adjuster specialization and amount bands

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Writing the SQL queries Now Im thinking through several validation checks computing z scores to flag anomalies in the transition timings examining the RP pattern for rigid scheduling by looking at the distribution of deltas and checking if events consistently occur at specific times of day verifying EN transitions happen within seconds or minutes with system resources involved identifying premature closures where C appears without preceding E or P events analyzing batching patterns of N events by minute buckets per resource and correlating claims across adjusters using resource names

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: I should also investigate missing adjuster links by parsing the additional_info field with regex patterns and check for duplicate approvals out of order events and whether PN delays align with business hours or weekends

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - IND
Text: Ill structure the final answer with clear headings and include a note on ZETA parameterization

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Theres also a strong data quality hypothesis worth exploring timestamps might be recorded at batch upload time all claim events inserted simultaneously or timezone misalignment could be causing issues

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: More importantly since submission_date is a DATE field R timestamps might be truncated to midnight which would artificially create a rigid RP offset when P runs on a nightly batch schedule

Name: Validation - PE
Text: I should write a query to verify this hypothesis about the midnight truncation effect

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Now Im checking the time of day distribution for both R and P events to see if theres a pattern that confirms the batch processing theory
